title: The Email I Almost Didn't Send

story.opening: <break time="400ms"/>I almost didn't send the email. <break time="350ms"/>The one asking for accommodations at work.
story.body: <break time="350ms"/>I wrote it and deleted it six times. <break time="300ms"/>Every version felt like too much. <break time="250ms"/>Or too little. <break time="400ms"/>I was terrified they'd see me as less capable. <break time="350ms"/>That asking for flexibility would mark me. <break time="300ms"/>That I'd become 'the sick employee.' <break time="500ms"/>But I was drowning. <break time="400ms"/>Pushing through was destroying me. <break time="350ms"/>So I hit send. <break time="300ms"/>And then I panicked for three hours.
story.reflection: <break time="500ms"/>They said yes. <break time="400ms"/>To everything. <break time="350ms"/>I cried at my desk. <break time="500ms"/>Not everyone's story goes that way. <break time="400ms"/>I know that. <break time="350ms"/>But asking for what you need isn't weakness. <break time="300ms"/>It's survival. <break time="500ms"/>The worst they can say is no. <break time="400ms"/>And you're already living in the consequences of not asking.

when_to_share: When someone is considering asking for workplace accommodations or disclosing their illness
